title: Cliometrics: Quantitative History | Anton Ebsen
description: Exploring the past through economic theory and econometric models. From the British fertility transition to structural transformation patterns.

hero.title: Where History Meets Data
hero.text: Cliometrics – or quantitative economic history – is the bridge between archival pasts and modern analytical tools. By applying rigorous econometric methods to historical datasets, we can isolate the causal mechanisms that shaped the modern world.

philosophy.title: My Approach
philosophy.text: My research is driven by the conviction that history is our best laboratory. By studying long-run patterns, we can better understand institutional persistence and today's economic development.
points: Quantitative Rigor: Supporting historical narratives with hard data.
Institutional Persistence: How past choices shape modern frameworks.
Reproducibility: Open access to code and historical datasets.

list.title: The Cost of Quality
list.desc: Analyzing how the 19th-century British Factory Acts accelerated the fertility transition by increasing the implicit cost of child labor.

list.title: Structural Transformation
list.desc: A visualization of three generations' economic journey in the context of Denmark's shift from agriculture to a welfare state.

toolkit.title: Clio's Toolkit
toolkit.desc: The methodological foundations and rich datasets that enable modern cliometric analysis.

items.name: Maddison Project
items.desc: The gold standard for long-term GDP time series, making it possible to compare prosperity across centuries.

items.name: IPUMS International
items.desc: Harmonized census microdata allowing for detailed studies of family and occupational structures over time.

items.name: Clio-Infra
items.desc: A comprehensive archive of global historical indicators for inequality, human capital, and institutions.

items.name: GeoPandas & GIS
items.desc: Integrating spatial data to understand the geographical dimension of historical economic shocks.

items.name: Populations Past
items.desc: Extraordinarily detailed demographic data from British censuses 1851-1911.

archivistLog.title: The Archivist's Log
archivistLog.desc: Insights into the complex work of transforming dusty archives into precise econometric data.

stories.title: The Factory Acts Puzzle
stories.text: To isolate the effect of 19th-century factory laws, I had to cross-reference textile districts with data on energy sources. The challenge was distinguishing between water-powered industry (early implementation) and steam-powered (later adoption).

stories.title: The Globalization Panel
stories.text: Building a unified panel of 32 OECD countries to analyze the impact of globalization. The challenge lay in harmonizing national statistics for social transfers, as definitions of 'welfare state' vary across borders.

gisGallery.title: GIS & Spatial Analysis
gisGallery.desc: Visualizing historical patterns through modern Geographical Information Systems.

maps.title: The British Fertility Transition
maps.desc: The map showing the geographical spread of declining fertility rates in the late 19th century, focusing on industrial clusters in Northern England.

maps.title: Globalization Impact
maps.desc: A spatial analysis of 'China Shock' exposure at the regional level in Europe, illustrating the inequality in globalization's consequences.

replication.title: Replication Dashboard
replication.desc: Transparency and reproducibility are fundamental to my research process. Access the code and data behind my published papers and working projects.

items.title: The Cost of Quality - Full Package

methodology.title: Identification Strategy
methodology.desc: How we isolate causality in historical settings.

caseStudy.title: Diff-in-Diffs in the 19th Century
caseStudy.context: The 1833 Factory Act limited child labor hours, but only in textile mills. This created a natural experiment where we can compare textile districts (Treatment) with other industrial districts (Control).
caseStudy.identification: By using a Difference-in-Differences estimator, I can control for time-invariant regional characteristics and common time shocks, isolating the Act's impact on family fertility choices.

library.title: The Reading Room
library.desc: A curated bibliography of the works that have most influenced my approach to cliometric history.

books.author: Goldin & Katz
books.title: The Race between Education and Technology
books.impact: Crucial for understanding how human capital formation drives long-run growth.

books.author: Douglass C. North
books.title: Institutions, Institutional Change and Economic Performance
books.impact: The theoretical foundation for why institutions matter more than geography.

books.author: Thomas Piketty
books.title: Capital in the Twenty-First Century
books.impact: A masterclass in using historical tax records to visualize long-run inequality.
